'''Rove''' was a large barren [[rogue moon]] situated in<ref name="CRO" /> the [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim's]]<ref name="TEA" /> [[Bramior system/Legends|Bramior system]]. Eons before the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], it had been [[Gravity/Legends|captured]] by the [[Bramior star]]. By the [[Time/Legends|time]] shortly after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], Rove was used in the game of [[Bombarde]], the main attraction of the luxury [[Gambling/Legends|gambling]] [[Cal-class battleship|starship]] ''[[Cal Ambre]]'' that also maintained a permanent [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] in the Bramior [[Star system/Legends|system]]. The [[Moon/Legends|moon]] would be overlaid with an electronic grid, after which the ''Cal Ambre'' would use its [[Mass-driver cannon/Legends|mass driver cannon]] to shoot an [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]]—drawn from the nearby [[Asteroid field/Legends|asteroid field]]—at randomly generated coordinates on the natural satellite's surface. Meanwhile, aboard the [[Kumauri Battleship|starship]], bets were placed on which section of the moon the projectile would impact, and the gamblers gathered to watch the event from a large observation gallery.<ref name="CRO" />

==Behind the scenes==
Rove was created by [[Paul Sudlow]] and was introduced in ''[[Cracken's Rebel Operatives]]'', a [[1994]] [[West End Games]] sourcebook published for use with ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="CRO" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Bramior system, and therefore Rove, in grid square M-9.<ref name="TEA" />
